NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GREEN TREE SERVICING, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADDRESS ALL ISSUES RAISED BY DEMURRER. ON MAY 16, 2013 COURT ORDERED PLAINTIFF TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.6B. COURT RECEIVED COPIES OF ONLY PAGE 1, PAGE 21 AND PROOF OF SERVICE STAPLED TOGETHER. MOVING PARTY IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.6C IN FUTURE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER CONFORMING TO THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT GREEN TREE SERVICING, LLC ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GREEN TREE SERVICING' LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE OR VACATE VOLUNTARY DISMISSAL AND TO DISMISS THE COMPLAINT FOR FAILURE TO AMEND WITHIN THE TIME ALLOWED BY THE COURT IS GRANTED. ONCE A GENERAL DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AND PLAINTIFFS DO NOT SO AMEND WITHIN THE TIME AUTHORIZED BY THE COURT OR OTHERWISE EXTENDED BY STIPULATION OR APPROPRIATE ORDER, THEY CAN NO LONGER VOLUNTARILY DISMISS THEIR ACTION PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 581, SUBDIVISION 1, EVEN IF THE TRIAL COURT HAS YET TO ENTER A JUDGMENT OF DISMISSAL ON THE SUSTAINED DEMURRER." (WELLS V. MARINA CITY PROPERTIES, INC. (1981) 29 CAL.3D 781, 789.) THE VOLUNTARY DISMISSAL ON AUGUST 1, 2013 WAS ACCEPTED IN ERROR AND IS VACATED. THE COMPLAINT IS DISMISSED FOR FAILURE TO AMEND. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK J. RUMSEY, NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Strike Or Vacate Voluntary Dismissal And To Dismiss The Complaint For Failure To Amend Within The Time Allowed By The Court
Off Calendar Nov-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT GREEN TREE SERVICING' LLC's Motion To Strike Or Vacate Voluntary Dismissal And To Dismiss The Complaint For Failure To Amend Within The Time Allowed By The Court is Granted. Once a general demurrer is sustained with leave to amend and Plaintiffs do not so amend within the time authorized by the court or otherwise extended by stipulation or appropriate order, they can no longer voluntarily dismiss their action pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 581, subdivision 1, even if the trial court has yet to enter a judgment of dismissal on the sustained demurrer." (Wells v. Marina City Properties, Inc. (1981) 29 Cal.3d 781, 789.) The voluntary dismissal on August 1, 2013 was accepted in error and is vacated. The Complaint is dismissed for failure to amend. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Or Vacate Voluntary Dismissal And To Dismiss The Complaint For Failure To Amend Within The Time Allowed By The Court
